name: Copenhagen
summary: Copenhagen is Denmark's capital, where Hans Christian Andersen wrote The Little Mermaid and The Ugly Duckling. You'll find Tivoli Gardens, the world's second-oldest amusement park, plus the famous Little Mermaid statue by the harbour. It's the bike capital of the world, and LEGO bricks were invented here too.
funFacts: Tivoli Gardens opened in 1843 and inspired Walt Disney when he was dreaming up Disneyland, so every Disney park has a little Copenhagen magic in it.
Copenhageners own around five times more bicycles than cars, and on busy days more bikes than cars roll through the city centre.
LEGO was invented in Denmark in 1932, and the name comes from two Danish words, 'leg godt', which mean 'play well'.
Hans Christian Andersen, born in Denmark, wrote The Little Mermaid, The Ugly Duckling, Thumbelina and The Princess and the Pea.
The Little Mermaid statue is tiny, only about 1.25 metres tall, and many visitors are surprised by how small the world-famous bronze girl really is.
thingsToDo: Spin through Tivoli Gardens, riding the wooden roller coaster from 1914 and the Flying Trunk ride that visits 32 Hans Christian Andersen fairy tales.
Hop on a canal boat from Nyhavn, gliding past the rainbow row of old sailor houses where Hans Christian Andersen once lived at number 67.
Walk along the harbour to meet the Little Mermaid statue, then explore the star-shaped Kastellet fortress with its grassy ramparts nearby.
Climb the Round Tower's curving brick ramp, wide enough for a horse, up to a rooftop platform with sweeping views over the old city.
Visit Rosenborg Castle to peek at Denmark's real crown jewels and royal swords, then run around the King's Garden park outside.
Pop into the giant LEGO flagship store on Stroget shopping street to build mini-figures and spot huge brick sculptures.

localWord.meaning: Hello (used by everyone, all day long)

images.alt: Rainbow-coloured townhouses line the Nyhavn canal with wooden sailing boats moored along the water.
Tivoli Gardens glows with thousands of fairy lights reflected on a calm lake at blue hour.
The red-brick Renaissance towers of Rosenborg Castle rise above its leafy royal gardens.
Copenhagen's 17th-century Round Tower stands tall above the cobbled streets of the old city.
Lots of people ride bikes through Kongens Nytorv square showing Copenhagen's bicycle culture.
